name: do-mpc (Model Predictive Control in Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (LGPL-3.0); Sebastian Lucia / Sergio Lucia / TU Berlin

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required; requires CasADi backend

performance.notes: CasADi + IPOPT/qpOASES/CPLEX backend; solution time depends on horizon and problem size

agent_readiness.notes: Model Predictive Control framework built on CasADi. import do_mpc. model = do_mpc.model.Model(model_type='continuous'). x = model.set_variable('_x', 'x', (2,1)). u = model.set_variable('_u', 'u', (1,1)). model.set_rhs('x', A @ x + B @ u). model.setup(). mpc = do_mpc.controller.MPC(model). mpc.set_param(n_horizon=20, t_step=0.05). mpc.set_objective(mterm=x.T @ Q @ x, lterm=u.T @ R @ u). mpc.setup(). Estimator: do_mpc.estimator.StateFeedback. Simulator: do_mpc.simulator.Simulator. Main loop: u0 = mpc.make_step(x0). Stochastic MPC. Robust MPC with scenario trees. Live plotting.

gotchas: CasADi required as backend — adds dependency complexity
Nonlinear MPC requires IPOPT solver — can be slow for fast systems
Model definition in CasADi symbolic math is verbose
Real-time MPC requires warm-starting and solver tuning
best_when: Nonlinear Model Predictive Control (NMPC) for chemical processes, robotics
Economic MPC and robust/stochastic MPC with scenario trees
When CasADi-based optimization is already in use
avoid_when: Linear MPC on simple systems (use control library or cvxpy)
Embedded real-time MPC (use ACADO or forcespro)
When MATLAB MPC Toolbox compatibility is required
summary: do-mpc is a Model Predictive Control framework using CasADi. Model: set_variable('_x'), set_rhs(). MPC: set_param(n_horizon=20). set_objective(mterm, lterm). make_step(x0) in control loop. Simulator and Estimator classes. Robust/stochastic MPC. Live plotting. LGPL-3.0 by TU Berlin. Best for nonlinear MPC research and process control. CasADi dependency. IPOPT for NLP. Verbose CasADi model definition. Not for embedded real-time.
